nodes.critic_note: gross deformity → emergency, checked before anything else

nodes.condition: pain_malleolar_zone is True and (bone_tenderness_lateral_malleolus is True or bone_tenderness_medial_malleolus is True or can_bear_weight is False)
nodes.critic_note: the Ottawa Ankle Rule — 5 interacting criteria that rule fracture in/out; this is the combinatorics a flat 'is it broken?' check misses
nodes.gray_zone: Ottawa Rules are validated for ages ~18–55; outside that, escalate (handled below)

nodes.critic_note: can't bear weight but Ottawa-negative (no malleolar pain) — still warrants in-person assessment

nodes.condition: age_years is not None and (age_years < 18 or age_years > 55)
nodes.critic_note: Ottawa Rules less validated under 18 / over 55 — be conservative

nodes.condition: hours_since_injury is not None and hours_since_injury <= 72
nodes.critic_note: OH-MERDE: the source skill said RICE + complete rest + heavy icing — that's outdated since ~2012. Acute phase is PEACE: Protect, Elevate, Avoid anti-inflammatory modalities incl. prolonged ICE (it slows healing), Compress, Educate — with early protected loading, NOT complete rest
nodes.gray_zone: ice for symptom relief is fine briefly; the harm is prolonged/aggressive icing and immobilization

nodes.condition: hours_since_injury is not None and hours_since_injury > 72
nodes.critic_note: subacute phase is LOVE: Load (progressive), Optimism, Vascularisation (gentle cardio), Exercise — mobilize, don't rest
